title: viola tricolor var. gracilescens
intro: Viola tricolor var. gracilescens, also known as Heart's Ease or Wild Pansy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the violet family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, as well as being used to calm stress and anxiety due to its sedative and antidepressant properties. The herbal preparations of Viola tricolor var. gracilescens include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent, and can be used externally or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
